content:
I am a Ph.D. student at NYU Tandon advised by Lisa Hellerstein and Chris Musco. I am interested in graph theory and probability query problems.
I received my B.A. in Mathematics and Computer Science from Middlebury College. At Middlebury, I designed quantum algorithms for graph theory problems with Shelby Kimmel and worked on applications of math in recreational board games with Alex Lyford.
summary:
I am a Ph.D. student at NYU Tandon advised by Lisa Hellerstein and Chris Musco. I am interested in graph theory and probability query problems.
I received my B.A. in Mathematics and Computer Science from Middlebury College.
title: R. Teal Witter

summary: We identify winning strategies for and improvements to Ticket to Ride using graph-theoretic and probabilistic concepts.
title: Applications of Graph Theory and Probability in the Board Game Ticket to Ride

summary: We present quantum algorithms for various problems related to graph connectivity.
title: Applications of the Quantum Algorithm for st-Connectivity
